MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To Amend Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-15-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Brenda Hunter's motion for leave to amend her complaint to seek punitive damages is denied without prejudice to refiling the motion. CCP 425.13(a) requires that this motion be supported by evidence showing that Ms. Hunter can prove her request for punitive damages. Because the motion is not supported by any evidence, the motion must be denied. Order signed.

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-20-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS CHRISTINA PHAM, MD, MARY CONNOLLY, MD, DARLENE LEE, NP, KENNETH SACK, MD, RENUKA NAYAK, MD and REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT AND TO EACH AND EVERY CAUSE OF ACTION THEREIN is SUSTAINED with thirty days leave to amend. The third amended complaint is uncertain and does not allege sufficient facts to state a claim for either intentional infliction of emotional distress or medical malpractice against any of the defendants. While alleged conduct in the nature of sexual harassment may state a claim for intentional infliction of emotional distress, the alleged conduct must be outrageous, the defendant must have acted with reckless disregard of the probability that the defendant would suffer emotional distress, and the plaintiff must have suffered severe emotional distress. There are insufficient facts as to each of these required elements. Ms. Hunter also failed to allege how the alleged medical malpractice of each of the defendants caused her compensable harm such that she is entitled to damages. To state a claim for medical malpractice, Ms. Hunter must allege precisely how the conduct of each defendant fell below the applicable standard of care and how she was harmed by that conduct. Ms. Hunter is given one last opportunity to correct the deficiencies in her claims. If she chooses to attempt to correct these deficiencies, Ms. Hunter's next complaint need not be lengthy or repetitive, but it does need to cover all the required elements of each claim as to each defendant against whom the claim is asserted. Written order is signed and filed in open court.
